title: Polestar speeds plans to make more cars outside China as EU-China tensions rise
text: Polestar accelerated its strategy to localize manufacturing outside China in response to escalating EU-China trade tensions. The company is expanding production capacity in South Carolina and Slovakia to reduce dependence on Chinese manufacturing. The Polestar 3 SUV production shifted from split Chengdu-South Carolina to exclusive South Carolina manufacturing. The Polestar 4 expanded beyond Hangzhou Bay to Busan, South Korea, through a partnership with Renault Korea. The Polestar 7 compact SUV, slated for 2028 launch, will be produced at Volvo's Slovakia plant rather than in China. This regionalization strategy aims to reduce tariff exposure, simplify supply chains, and qualify for regional tax incentives and subsidies. By producing in the US, South Korea, and Europe, Polestar can better serve regional markets while reducing transportation costs and tariff burdens. The strategy also provides supply chain resilience amid geopolitical uncertainty. Management stated that by 2028, the majority of Polestar's vehicles will be manufactured outside of China. This shift represents a significant strategic pivot from initial reliance on Chinese manufacturing to a more globally distributed production footprint aligned with regional market demand.
